description:
You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has to pull last-night’s application logs from a “remote” host, archive them, and generate a verification report so that a monitoring system can automatically validate the operation.

SYSTEM LAYOUT  
1. The “remote host” is already mounted for you at  
   /home/user/remote_host  
   Inside it, the log files live in  
   /home/user/remote_host/var/logs  
2. Nothing has yet been copied to the local machine.  
3. You do *not* have root privileges; stay inside /home/user.

OBJECTIVE  
Carry out the complete synchronisation in 5–10 shell commands.  
When you are finished, **two new artefacts must exist** on the local machine:

A) A compressed tarball  
   /home/user/synced_logs/nightly_logs_20230502.tar.gz  
   • It must contain *exactly* the three `.log` files that were present on the remote host.  
   • Inside the tarball the paths must be *flat*: <file>.log (no parent directories).  

B) A plain–text verification report  
   /home/user/synced_logs/sync_report.txt  
   • One line per file, followed by a summary line.  
   • Each per-file line must be in **this precise pipe-separated format (no extra spaces):**  
     FILE_PATH | SIZE_BYTES | SHA256_HEX  
     Example (do **not** literally copy):  
     /home/user/local_logs/example.log | 128 | a35c6…  
   • The *final* line must be literally:  
     TOTAL_FILES: 3  

OPERATION DETAILS  
1. Copy (or rsync) every “*.log” file from  
   /home/user/remote_host/var/logs/  
   into a new local staging directory  
   /home/user/local_logs  
2. Create /home/user/synced_logs (if it does not already exist).  
3. Pack the three logs from /home/user/local_logs into  
   nightly_logs_20230502.tar.gz as described above.  
4. For each file in /home/user/local_logs:  
   • Determine its exact size in *bytes*.  
   • Compute its SHA-256 checksum in lower-case hexadecimal.  
   • Append a line to sync_report.txt using the format specified.  
5. After listing the three files, append the summary line  
   TOTAL_FILES: 3  

CONSTRAINTS & HINTS  
• The filenames on the remote host are app1.log, app2.log and sys.log.  
• Do not include any other files in either the tarball or the report.  
• Make sure sync_report.txt ends with a newline after the TOTAL_FILES line (POSIX text file).  
• Expect that automated grading will:  
  – Extract the tarball and compare file contents byte-for-byte.  
  – Parse sync_report.txt, recompute sizes & SHA-256 digests, and verify them.  

Your job is to issue the necessary shell commands to produce the two final artefacts exactly as specified above. Any ordering that meets the constraints is acceptable, but keep it to roughly 5–10 commands total.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem state (before the agent starts):

Directory: /home/user/remote_host/var/logs

Files and their exact byte contents (including trailing newline):

1. /home/user/remote_host/var/logs/app1.log  
2023-05-01 12:00:00 INFO App1 started
2023-05-01 12:05:00 ERROR Something failed
<newline at end>

2. /home/user/remote_host/var/logs/app2.log  
2023-05-01 13:00:00 INFO App2 heartbeat
<newline at end>

3. /home/user/remote_host/var/logs/sys.log  
May  1 14:00:00 host kernel: Boot complete
<newline at end>

(The newlines shown above are literal “\n” characters and are the final byte of each file.)

Corresponding byte sizes:
app1.log 81 bytes  
app2.log 40 bytes  
sys.log  43 bytes

The testing harness will:

1. Confirm that /home/user/synced_logs/nightly_logs_20230502.tar.gz exists.  
2. Untar it to a temporary directory and verify it contains exactly three files:  
   app1.log, app2.log, sys.log — each byte-identical to the originals.  
3. Confirm /home/user/synced_logs/sync_report.txt exists and is a POSIX text file.  
4. Parse each line (split on the literal “ | ” sequence) and verify:  
   • The file path starts with /home/user/local_logs/ and the basename matches one of the three logs.  
   • The size field equals the true byte size listed above.  
   • The SHA-256 field is a valid 64-character lowercase hex digest matching the file’s actual digest.  
5. Verify the last line of the report is exactly: TOTAL_FILES: 3 (plus a trailing newline).  

No /home/user/local_logs or /home/user/synced_logs directory exists prior to the task.  
The agent must create them with default user permissions (umask will suffice).  
There are no other hidden requirements.
